widgets_values: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly

nodes.title: Prompt
widgets_values: Egyptian royal in blue-and-gold headdress and high collar, white dress with golden embroidery and armbands, desert, robot soldiers in formation left and right. She walks steadily forward, head held level and gaze fixed ahead—no dipping or lowering of the head. The camera performs a single, smooth push-in only: starting in a wider shot of her, the robots, and the desert, it moves steadily forward until she is in a medium or medium-close frame, then holds. She stops, posture and head still upright, and says: “The old gods are silent. I am not.” Robot soldiers shift or march in place; sand and fabric move with the wind. No pull-back; the only camera move is the continuous push-in.

inputs.text: blurry, low quality, still frame, frames, watermark, overlay, titles, has blurbox, has subtitles

nodes.title: Model Links
widgets_values:
- Huggingface: [Lightricks/LTX-2.3](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/)
- Github: [LTX-2](https://github.com/Lightricks/LTX-2)

## LTX-2.3 I2V With Expert LoRAs

This workflow is based on ComfyUI's default LTX-2.3 Image-to-Video template, with prompt enhancement removed. The prompt text is encoded directly, and two model-only Expert LoRA slots are chained after the distilled LoRA so both LTX sampling stages use the same expert stack. Defaults are tuned for a realistic 48 GB FP8 run that should stay cache-friendly between generations on a normal ComfyUI setup: 768x512 output, 49 frames, FP8 checkpoint, FP4 text encoder, and tiled VAE decode. This workflow cannot force ComfyUI to keep models resident if your launch flags or memory settings make it unload models; if you still see model reloads between queued runs, lower the output size/length or use ComfyUI normal/high-VRAM settings instead of low-VRAM/offload modes.

Use the Expert LoRA widgets for LoRAs produced by `lora.noise_experts`, for example:

- `lora_weights_low_noise_step_02000.safetensors`
- `lora_weights_high_noise_step_02000.safetensors`

Copy your trained expert LoRAs into `ComfyUI/models/loras`, then choose the filenames in `expert_lora_1` and `expert_lora_2` and tune each strength. Leave a slot at strength `0.0` if you want to disable it while keeping the workflow wired. If you have extra headroom and the model stack stays resident, raise length to 81 or 97 frames; keep values at `8n + 1`. The original 1280x720, 121-frame two-stage template is not a realistic fast target for 48 GB once extra expert LoRAs are added.

## LTX-2.3 Prompting Tips

1. **Core Actions**: Describe events and actions as they occur over time
2. **Visual Details**: Describe all visual details you want to appear in the video
3. **Audio**: Describe sounds and dialogue needed for the scene

## Model Links (for Local Users)

**checkpoints**

- [ltx-2.3-22b-dev-fp8.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3-fp8/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-dev-fp8.safetensors)

**loras**

- [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384.safetensors)
- Your trained expert LoRAs from ltx-trainer

**latent_upscale_models**

- [ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors)

## Model Storage Location

```
ComfyUI/
├── models/
│   ├── checkpoints/
│   │   └── ltx-2.3-22b-dev-fp8.safetensors
│   ├── loras/
│   │   ├── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-384.safetensors
│   │   ├── lora_weights_low_noise_step_02000.safetensors
│   │   └── lora_weights_high_noise_step_02000.safetensors
│   └── latent_upscale_models/
│       └── ltx-2.3-spatial-upscaler-x2-1.1.safetensors
```

## Report Issue

Note: Please update ComfyUI first ([guide](https://docs.comfy.org/installation/update_comfyui)) and prepare required models. Desktop/Cloud updates follow stable releases, so some nightly-supported models may not be available yet.

- Cannot run / runtime errors: [ComfyUI/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI/issues)
- UI / frontend issues: [ComfyUI_frontend/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI_frontend/issues)
- Workflow issues: [workflow_templates/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates/issues)
